system.description:
This skill indicates the ability of a character to ride horses, mules, donkeys, or burros. With just a 15% rating in the skill (or 5% for mules and burros), the character is able to stay mounted without too much trouble, as long as the animal travels at a walk; but he makes Ride rolls to avoid having it rear up, to keep it calm if engaged in combat, if he wants it to travel at a trot or gallop, or to make it perform some sort of jump or other such stunt. Failure at the roll means that the rider can't control the mount, and he will fall off if he rolls a second failure or a blunder. Falling from a moving horse is equivalent to falling from a height of 3 varas, meaning the rider receives 1D6 Damage Points.

For those who ride to battle on horseback, as mentioned in Chapter IV (page 130), fighting from horseback is a Combined Roll (see page 77) of Ride and his weapon skill.
